I've been studying copy for about 15 years now And I've gone through lots of insights and testing and testing on email and facebook ads and paid search and things like that So I've learned a ton over time that I can take any avatar and literally create copy within 10 minutes And you know, if I was a copywriter I could I could easily charge thousands and I can make a career out of that But you know, I enjoy doing more than just writing copy But the construct is really simple This is something that's been in the marketing fold For decades and decades way before the internet was even made Which is the ADA funnel attention interest desire and action Um, I can't tell you how simple this is because it ties into the 5w avatar So the first part of ADA is attention So the big bold statement reach for their problem or opportunity the first two lines in your copy matter And and you know, this will depend on the ad format you're playing with but for example in the news feed If you can't hook them on the image in the first two lines, the rest of it doesn't matter So that's really really important to get that right and you don't need to grab that attention through Attention comes through the visual as well as the copy depending on the ad format Then comes the interest stage So you've got them hooked now keep them build a bridge story from problem to solution So the bridge story I used here was that I I wanted to find the perfect gif But I wanted something that shared my personal message to her and and you know bringing the problem to solution Was that I found a necklace where I could actually personalize it and I personalize it So now I I step them from interest to desire.
